subject: Ecology
title: Forest Hydrology
description: A quick win: understand Forest Hydrology.

hook.text: Forests don't just grow in rain—they actually help create it.

buildup.text: Trees pull water from soil and release vapor through transpiration—billions of liters daily.

discovery.text: That vapor rises, condenses, and falls again as rain downwind—a 'flying river.'

twist.text: The Amazon recycles its own rainfall so effectively it rains more inside the forest than out.

climax.text: Deforestation breaks this cycle, reducing rainfall and drying out once-wet regions.

punchline.text: Cut the trees and you cut the rain.

quiz.question: How do forests influence rainfall?
options: Trees release vapor that forms new rain downwind
